title: Fomite Transmission Nuance
script_summary: Exploring the complexities of fomite transmission with a focus on scientific clarity.

segments.voiceover: Science has a clearer story about fomite transmission nuance than social media does.
Fomites are surfaces that can carry viruses. Think doorknobs, keyboards, even your phone.
But how contagious are these surfaces? It's not as simple as you might think.
Studies show that the virus can survive on surfaces for hours or even days, but the risk of transmission decreases over time.
So, touching a surface doesn’t guarantee infection. It’s a complex interplay of factors.
Want to stay informed? Follow for more science insights!
